      takes a MMAP with K types and a probability matrix with element PROB(k,s) giving the probability that a type-k arrival should be marked as a class-s arrival and returns a new MMAP that has R classes as output types.

      • mmap_exponential

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_exponential​(Matrix lambda,
                                                         int n)
        fits a order-n MMAP with given arrival rates lambda
        Parameters:
        lambda - 1 by k Matrix describing the arrival rates of each jobclass
        n - numbers of MMAP states
        Returns:
        MMAP

      • mmap_exponential

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_exponential​(Matrix lambda)

      • mmap_normalize

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_normalize​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP)
        Fixes MMAP feasibility by setting negative values to zero and forcing the other conditions.
        Parameters:
        MMAP - MMAP to be normalized
        Returns:
        normalized MMAP

      • mmap_mark

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_mark​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP,
                                                  Matrix prob)
        takes a MMAP with K types and a probability matrix with element PROB(k,s) giving the probability that a type-k arrival should be marked as a class-s arrival and returns a new MMAP that has R classes as output types.
        Parameters:
        MMAP - the original MMAP with K tpyes
        prob - a K by S matrix describing the probability of a type-k arrival in original MMAP tp be marked as a type-S arrival in the new MMAP
        Returns:
        a new MMAP with S tpyes

      • mmap_scale

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_scale​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP,
                                                   Matrix M)
        Changes the mean inter-arrival time of an MMAP.
        Parameters:
        MMAP - MMAP to be scaled
        M - new mean
        Returns:
        scaled MMAP

      • mmap_count_lambda

        public static Matrix mmap_count_lambda​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap)
        Computes the arrival rate of the counting process, for the given Marked MAP.
        Parameters:
        mmap - the Marked MAP
        Returns:
        the vector with the rate for each job class

      • mmap_lambda

        public static Matrix mmap_lambda​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP)
        same as mmap_count_lambda
        Parameters:
        MMAP -
        Returns:

      • mmap_super

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_super​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAPa,
                                                   Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAPb,
                                                   String opt)
        Combine two MMAPs in to one sup MMAP
        Parameters:
        MMAPa - MMAP 1
        MMAPb - MMAP 2
        opt - "default" or "match", "default" treats two MMAPs are irrelevant and each class in MMAPa and MMAPb is a distinct class in SUP "match" requires MMAPa and MMAPb have the same number of classes and class c in both MMAPa and MMAPb is mapped both into class c of SUP
        Returns:

      • mmap_super

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_super​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAPa,
                                                   Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAPb)

      • mmap_super

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_super​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAPa)

      • mmap_hide

        public static Map<Integer,​Matrix> mmap_hide​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP,
                                                  Matrix types)
        Makes a subset of the MMAP type hidden. takes a MMAP with K arrival types and % returns a new MMAP for the same stochastic process with those arrivals % hidden from observation.
        Parameters:
        MMAP - the original MMAP
        types - types to be hidden
        Returns:

      • mmap_maps

        public static Map<Integer,​Map<Integer,​Matrix>> mmap_maps​(Map<Integer,​Matrix> MMAP)
        Returns K MAPs, one for each class of the MMAP[K] process.
        Parameters:
        MMAP - MMAP
        Returns:
        K MAPs
